hero.tagline: India's first strong-hybrid MPV. More space, more efficiency, more premium.

pricing.notes: Ex-showroom prices based on publicly listed variant prices. Verify with Toyota dealer for current on-road pricing.

overview.minides: India's benchmark premium MPV now with strong hybrid efficiency.
overview.description: The Toyota Innova HyCross is a monocoque-platform premium MPV replacing the Innova Crysta. Built on the TNGA-C platform, it offers a 2.0L petrol CVT for base trims (G/GX) and a 2.0L strong hybrid e-CVT for upper trims (VX/ZX). Available in 7 or 8-seater configurations, 12 variants across 7 trim families. Top ZX(O) gets full Level 2 ADAS, 9-speaker JBL, HUD, ventilated seats, powered Ottoman seats, and panoramic sunroof. 5-star Bharat NCAP rated with standard 7 airbags including driver knee airbag across all variants.

dimensions.bootSpace: 213 Litres (3rd row up) / 867 Litres (3rd row folded)

brakes.note: All-wheel disc brakes standard across all variants

hybrid_2_0_atkinson.name: 2.0L Atkinson Cycle Petrol + Electric (Strong Hybrid)
hybrid_2_0_atkinson.engineType: 2.0L DOHC Atkinson Cycle + Permanent Magnet Electric Motor

variants.name: G Fleet 7STR
keyFeatures: Rear AC vents with blower control

variants.name: ZX Hybrid
variants.notes: ZX Hybrid is available in 7-seater configuration only. ZX(O) adds ADAS.

variants.name: ZX(O) Hybrid
keyFeatures: Lane Departure Warning + Lane Keep Assist
Blind Spot Monitor + Rear Cross-Traffic Alert
variants.notes: ZX(O) is 7-seater only. Top-of-the-line Innova Hycross variant with full Level 2 ADAS.

blog.title: Toyota Innova HyCross review: India's best premium MPV
blog.subtitle: Monocoque platform, strong hybrid efficiency, 7 airbags standard — this is the Innova reimagined.
blog.summary: The Innova HyCross is a complete departure from the Crysta. Built on TNGA-C, it drives more like a car while offering significantly more space. The hybrid system is the real showstopper, returning over 20 kmpl in real-world city driving.

sections.heading: Platform & Engineering
body: Toyota moved from a ladder-frame to a monocoque platform, fundamentally improving ride, handling and NVH. The front-wheel-drive layout frees up more cabin space compared to the Crysta's rear-wheel-drive setup.

sections.heading: Hybrid System
body: The strong hybrid uses a 2.0L Atkinson-cycle petrol engine paired with a NiMH battery and permanent magnet motor. It can run in EV mode at low speeds and seamlessly switches to petrol or a combination. The e-CVT has no traditional gears — drive is silky smooth.

sections.heading: Features
body: Even the base G Fleet gets 7 airbags including a knee airbag — a segment benchmark. Top ZX(O) adds full Level 2 ADAS, JBL audio, HUD, ventilated seats and powered Ottoman second-row seats, making it one of the most feature-rich mass-market MPVs in India.

pros: TNGA platform — far superior to Crysta's ladder frame
Only full ADAS MPV under ₹32 lakh
cons: G and GX variants are petrol only (no hybrid)
blog.verdict: The Innova HyCross ZX Hybrid is the sweet spot for premium MPV buyers. The hybrid pays for itself in fuel savings over time. For those on a budget, the GX(O) Petrol is well-equipped at a sensible price.

faqs.question: How many variants does the Innova HyCross have?
faqs.answer: 12 variants across 7 trim families: G Fleet (7/8S), GX (7/8S), GX(O) (7/8S), VX Hybrid (7/8S), VX(O) Hybrid (7/8S), ZX Hybrid (7S only), ZX(O) Hybrid (7S only).

faqs.question: Does the Innova HyCross have ADAS?
faqs.answer: Yes, but only on the top-spec ZX(O) Hybrid variant. It gets Level 2 ADAS with FCW, AEB, LDW, LKA, BSM, RCTA, ACC and auto high beam.

faqs.question: What is the mileage of Innova HyCross?
faqs.answer: Petrol CVT variants return 16.13 kmpl (ARAI). Hybrid variants return 23.24 kmpl for 7-seaters and 23.23 kmpl for 8-seaters.

metadata.notes: All prices are ex-showroom Delhi. G Fleet variants are primarily sold to fleet/taxi operators but are also available for retail. ZX and ZX(O) are 7-seat only.
